name: What should I do if my content still doesn’t appear in AI overviews after I’ve optimized it?
description: Start by checking crawl/index status and comparing your content to pages already cited in overviews. Improve structure, depth, and clarity where you’re weaker,

mainEntity.name: What should I do if my content still doesn’t appear in AI overviews after I’ve optimized it?

acceptedAnswer.text:
If your content isn’t showing up in AI overviews yet, it usually means either search engines don’t see it as the best short answer or they haven’t recrawled and reassessed it since your changes.

You troubleshoot by checking technical access, competitive content, and whether you’ve truly answered the key questions clearly.

Key factors:
- Confirm pages are crawlable, indexable, and error-free in Search Console.
- Compare your pages to those cited in existing AI overviews for the same query.
- Look for unclear structure, missing FAQs, or shallow coverage.
- Check whether your updated pages have been recrawled and reindexed.

Create a simple diagnostic checklist and apply it to non-performing pages, then prioritize improvements that close obvious gaps versus competitors. Re‑submit improved URLs in Search Console and give them a few weeks.

An external audit can quickly surface structural or trust issues that are easy to miss from the inside.
acceptedAnswer.description: Start by checking crawl/index status and comparing your content to pages already cited in overviews. Improve structure, depth, and clarity where you’re weaker, resubmit key URLs, and allow several more weeks for recrawling and reprocessing.
